claims.text: In 2009 US Strategic Command released CONPLAN 8888-11, 'Counter-Zombie Dominance Operations,' a 31-page document laying out five operational phases for responding to a zombie apocalypse.

evidence.quote:
2009, United
States Strategic Command released Con
Plan 8888-11,
Counter Zombie Dominance Operations. 31
pages of detailed military strategy for
surviving a zombie apocalypse

claims.rationale: CONPLAN 8888-11 is a real US Strategic Command document, publicly available in PDF form since its 2011 FOIA release. All structural details (page count, 2009 origin, title) match the declassified document.

claims.text: CONPLAN 8888 was declassified in 2011 via a FOIA request and explicitly states it was kept unclassified 'to ensure maximum utility during times of crisis'.

evidence.quote:
declassified in 2011 thanks
to a foyer request

This document is
unclassified to ensure maximum utility

claims.rationale: FOIA release and the quoted unclassified-rationale language are both present in the public CONPLAN 8888 PDF. Verifiable against the document itself.

claims.text: CONPLAN 8888 classifies zombie threats into categories including pathogenic zombies (virus/bacteria/prion), symbiont-induced zombies (parasites), radiation zombies, weaponized zombies, space zombies, and evil magic zombies.

evidence.quote:
organized zombies
into distinct categories. According to
comp plan 8888, the most likely threat
comes from pathogenic zombies

claims.rationale: The seven-category zombie taxonomy (PZ, EZ, SZ, EMZ, WZ, SIZ, RZ, CZ) is explicitly enumerated in the actual CONPLAN 8888 document. Definitional claim about what the document contains.

claims.text: Prion diseases like kuru, mad-cow disease, and Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease can be transmitted by eating infected brain tissue and produce rapid mental deterioration plus aggression resembling zombie symptoms.

evidence.quote:
Eat a pron diseased brain and you become
infected

claims.rationale: Prion biology (kuru, vCJD/mad-cow, CJD) is well-established pathology. Transmission by ingesting contaminated neural tissue is documented in Papua New Guinea kuru cases and UK BSE/vCJD outbreak literature.

claims.text: The Ophiocordyceps fungus turns ants into 'zombie ants' by making them abandon their colony, climb high branches, bite down in a death grip, and release spores from their head onto the colony below.

evidence.quote:
fungus turns ants into zombie ants.
Infected ants abandon their colonies and
climb high branches. They bite down on
leaves with a death grip. The fungus
then consumes the ants body and burst
from its

claims.rationale: Ophiocordyceps unilateralis behavior is extensively documented in peer-reviewed entomology literature (Hughes 2011, etc.). Every detail of the host's description matches published observations.

claims.text: Between 30% and 50% of all humans carry Toxoplasma gondii, a single-celled parasite that may alter host behavior toward increased risk-taking.
claims.rationale: Global T. gondii seroprevalence varies widely (10-80% depending on country); 30-50% is a reasonable mid-range average. The 'risk-taking behavior change' is supported in rodent models and some human observational studies (e.g., Flegr) but remains contested in humans. directTruth moderate.

claims.text: CONPLAN 8888 explicitly names evil-magic zombies (EMZs) and assigns the Chaplain Corps as primary defense, implying the Pentagon takes supernatural threats seriously enough to require divine intervention.
claims.rationale: The EMZ section and Chaplain Corps reference are both literally in the document — factual on the surface. But reading this as 'the Pentagon takes supernatural threats seriously' misrepresents the document's own framing: the host himself concludes in the verdict that CONPLAN 8888 is a training exercise where evil-magic zombies are a stand-in for avoiding real-enemy references. directTruth low on the speculative framing.

claims.text: The military developed a covert psychological operation that trained millions of civilians in zombie-apocalypse survival protocols through entertainment like The Walking Dead, World War Z, and The Last of Us.

evidence.quote:
military developed a psychological
operation that trained millions of
civilians

claims.rationale: Host presents this as the central conspiracy thesis, then explicitly rejects it in the verdict section: zombies predate CONPLAN by 80+ years (White Zombie 1932, Night of the Living Dead 1968, Walking Dead comics 2003), so timing is coincidence and Hollywood-follows-money. directTruth near zero per the host's own verdict.

claims.text: CONPLAN 8888 is exactly what the Pentagon says it is — a training exercise where junior officers practice joint-planning procedures using an impossible scenario to avoid diplomatic problems with real countries.

evidence.quote:
comp plan 888 is exactly
what the Pentagon says it is, a training
exercise. Junior officers created it to
learn planning procedures. Using an
impossible scenario avoids diplomatic
problems

claims.rationale: This is the official US STRATCOM explanation publicly confirmed by the authors of the document (notably Tierney). The training-vehicle framing is consistent with the document's own disclaimer. Host accepts it in his verdict.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] If CONPLAN 8888 is just a joint-planning training exercise, there's no hidden PSYOP requiring zombie-media 'training' of civilians.
[alternative] The Chaplain-Corps / EMZ language fits a practice document's dry humor better than it fits a Pentagon supernatural-threat assessment.

claims.text: The zombie-genre timing argument (that CONPLAN 8888's 2009 release aligns with The Walking Dead's 2010 premiere) fails because White Zombie (1932), Romero's Night of the Living Dead (1968), and Walking Dead comics (2003) all predate the document.

evidence.quote:
White Zombie came out in 1932. George
Romero created modern zombies with Night

claims.rationale: All three dates are documented film/comics history. The genre timeline robustly predates CONPLAN 8888's 2009 authoring, which structurally defeats the 'psyop timed to zombie media' hypothesis.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] Zombie-media coordination requires causal timing the historical record precludes — zombies were a 77-year-old genre when CONPLAN 8888 was written.

claims.text: The Walking Dead TV series premiered on October 31, 2010 — the same year CONPLAN 8888 finished development.

evidence.quote:
The Walking Dead premiered on October
31st, 2010

claims.rationale: The Walking Dead pilot aired Oct 31 2010 on AMC — verifiable historical fact.

claims.text: CONPLAN 8888 repeatedly references classified annexes (space control, special technical operations, classified logistics) that were stripped from the public version but whose existence is acknowledged in the released text.

evidence.quote:
repeatedly references classified annexes
that were stripped from the public
version

claims.rationale: The released CONPLAN 8888 PDF does reference annexes (A through D+) that are not included in the public release. Footnotes pointing to classified sections are visible in the declassified copy. Whether those annexes contain genuinely secret capabilities vs boilerplate is unknown.

claims.text: CONPLAN 8888's Phase 3 locks down US military forces to fortified positions for at least 40 days while civilian areas are abandoned to local law enforcement.

evidence.quote:
Upon receipt of a US Stratcom order, all
forces will begin preparations to
conduct combat operations against zombie
threats. US Stratcom will shelter all
personnel in place for at least 40 days

claims.rationale: The 40-day shelter-in-place phase is directly quoted from the document. Host's characterization of civilian abandonment ('no civilian assistance, no rescue missions, no exceptions') is an accurate paraphrase of the document's silence on civilian protection post-lockdown.

claims.text: Zombies are not a literal military threat; CONPLAN 8888 is not evidence that the Pentagon expects an actual zombie outbreak.

evidence.quote:
zombie
scup theory also doesn't hold up

claims.rationale: Host's explicit verdict. No scientifically credible pathway to literal reanimation of the dead exists; the document's own unclassified-for-training framing confirms the training-exercise reading.

dependencies.rationale: The training-exercise framing is the mechanism that explains why a zombie plan exists without a real zombie threat.
